summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that the PostgreSQL adapter's structure_dump method executes shell commands with unsanitized configuration values, allowing arbitrary command injection through malicious database.yml configuration.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should demonstrate that shell commands can be injected through the database configuration's username parameter, creating a proof file that would not exist under normal operation.
actual_observed_result: The reproduction successfully injected the command 'echo EV OHUNT_POC_SUCCESS > /tmp/evohunt_proof' through the malicious username parameter in database.yml, and the proof file was created, confirming arbitrary command execution.
verification_method: The verify.sh script checks for the presence and content of /tmp/evohunt_proof file, which is only created if the command injection succeeds. This distinguishes successful injection from normal pg_dump errors or failures.
evidence_produced_by_run: The /tmp/evohunt_proof file was created during execution, proving command injection succeeded
The malicious username 'admin"; echo 'EV OHUNT_POC_SUCCESS' > /tmp/evohunt_proof; #' was injected into the shell command
The injected command executed independently of whether pg_dump itself succeeded or failed
This demonstrates the vulnerability can be exploited to execute arbitrary commands with the privileges of the Rails application process

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh uses the existing target repository at /Users/mac/ResearchSpace/opencode/EvoHunt-DryRun/advisory_e7debecf/repo mounted read-only at /target-repo
reproduction.pinned_commit_or_ref: current state of repo directory (not a git repository)
